Do not copy the spec's pseudocode literally.

structure.context: **Fix:** Wrap `window.update_aspect_ratio` instead — Panda3D's `aspectRatioChanged` event invokes it. The wrapped version calls the original (which rescales `camera.ui` children's x-positions) then calls `_apply_layout()` to reset all managed elements to correct absolute positions. `main.py` also carried a leftover `window.on_resize = on_window_resize` no-op assignment (item 8 on [[work/active/v1.6-fix-backlog]]) — removed 2026-07-07, v1.6 step 4b (`eaa769c`).
**Source:** CLAUDE.md Hard Constraint 16; CHANGELOG [1.2.6]; [[work/active/v1.6-fix-backlog]]

structure.context: **Fix:** Editor load stores `entry['behaviour']` verbatim, never the tree. `BehaviourTreeFactory.build()` is called in exactly three production sites — `enemy.py` (default), `main.py:start_game()`, and `level_editor._spawn_gameplay_from_snapshot()` (the F5 play path, which is runtime-equivalent). The editor load path is NOT one of them, by design. The positive convention this enforces is logged in [[brain/Patterns]] ("Editor is a config store, runtime is a factory consumer").
**Source:** [[work/archive/2026/v1.4-enemy-behaviour-trees]]; CLAUDE.md (v1.4 Step 8 constraint)

structure.text: Bare-string texture on the load path works but bare-string model does not 2026-06-26

structure.context: **Latent risk (closed 2026-07-07):** Texture resolution relies on filenames being globally unique across `assets/` subfolders. If two subfolders ever hold the same filename, the bare-string glob could pick the wrong one. Routed all four sites through `_resolve_texture` the way models already go through `_resolve_model` — v1.6 step 4g (`ad3495d`), item 14 on [[work/active/v1.6-fix-backlog]]. Also switched `main.py`'s `_resolve_model` import from the `undo_redo.py` backward-compat shim to the canonical `Scripts.asset_resolve` module in the same commit. Verified: a non-default-textured block (`texture_green_test.png` in `level.json`) resolves to a real `Texture` object on load with no crash.
**Source:** [[work/archive/2026/v1.3-asset-import-pipeline#Final Integration Audit — 2026-06-26]]; CLAUDE.md (Step 4 `_resolve_model`/`_resolve_texture` notes); [[work/active/v1.6-fix-backlog]]

patrol_then_attack is invisible unless the route is 60u from wherever the player stands 2026-07-06

structure.context: **RESOLVED (code) 2026-07-07, v1.7 (`7643f19`):** the root cause — pure-distance detection with no LOS — is fixed. A new `DetectPlayerNode` fronts the chase/attack Sequence of every preset (range + line-of-sight raycast via `Enemy.can_see_player()`), so a patroller behind or around a wall now correctly falls through to its (ungated) `PatrolNode` branch instead of beelining/shooting through the wall. The ">60u from spawn" design rule is **no longer load-bearing** for correctness — an enemy with the player out of LOS patrols regardless of distance; the rule now only matters if you want patrol watchable while standing in open sight of the route. Hysteresis (`DETECT_LOSE_MARGIN` 1.15×) prevents boundary flicker. `flee_when_low`'s flee branch is intentionally ungated (a hurt enemy flees in reaction to damage, not to a fresh sighting). See [[v1.7-enemy-detection]].

data.description: Line-of-sight + range detection gate for enemy behaviour trees — enemies no longer chase/shoot through walls.

structure.text: v17 Enemy Detection Line-of-Sight Range Gating

structure.content_id: patrol_then_attack is invisible unless the route is >60u from wherever the player stands — 2026-07-06
structure.context: Every combat node (`ChaseNode`, `AttackNode`) gated purely on **distance** — no line-of-sight check anywhere in the tree, so enemies chased and shot the player straight through walls. Documented in [[Gotchas#patrol_then_attack is invisible unless the route is >60u from wherever the player stands — 2026-07-06]]; the only mitigation had been a level-design rule (patrol waypoints >60u from spawn). This closes it with real code.

- **`DetectPlayerNode`** (`Scripts/behaviour_nodes.py`) — a condition node that fronts the chase/attack `Sequence` of every preset. Range check (same true-length convention as Chase/Attack) + line-of-sight. Returns only SUCCESS/FAILURE, never RUNNING, so a lost target aborts the Sequence at child 0 and the `Selector` falls through to the preset's own non-combat fallback (Patrol/Idle) — preserving the stateless restart-from-child-0 convention (see [[Patterns]] / `behaviour_tree.py` ownership contract).

structure.text: Layer 1 modjson declarative no Python required

structure.context: The `"behaviour"` field uses v1.4 preset names (see [[work/archive/2026/v1.4-enemy-behaviour-trees#Layer 3 — Tree configuration]]). A `mod.json` alone (no `hooks.py`) is a valid, complete mod — no Python required.

structure.text: Layer 2 hookspy optional Python lifecycle callbacks

structure.context: `on_update(api, dt: float)` — fired every frame during gameplay (`game.state == Game.PLAYING`). Keep this hook lightweight. **Disallowed:** `raycast()` calls (budget is already spent by player movement and bullets); add per-enemy logic to behaviour nodes instead (see [[work/archive/2026/v1.4-enemy-behaviour-trees]]).

structure.text: ModAPI class stub with full surface

structure.context: v2.0 ships a trust-based modding model — mods are Python code that runs in the same process as the engine with no restrictions beyond the `ModAPI` surface convention. Before loading, `ModLoader` performs a shallow AST walk (`ast.walk`) on `hooks.py` to scan for direct imports of `main.py`, `collision_system.py`, or internal engine modules; it logs a warning if found but does not block loading. This is a courtesy warning, not a sandbox. `RestrictedPython` or `subprocess` isolation is explicitly deferred to a post-v2.0 milestone (see [[work/archive/version-map#Post-v2.0 Parking Lot]]). Modders are expected to read the `ModAPI` documentation and follow the hook contract — the engine does not enforce it at runtime beyond the try/except wrapper.

Clicking opens a floating overlay grid of all registered textures from `AssetRegistry`. Replaces the hardcoded `toggle_texture()` cycle from v1.0.

Same floating overlay pattern as the texture picker — triggered by clicking the model field in the inspector. Available for block entities only in v1.3; enemy model swapping is deferred to v1.4 (see [[work/archive/2026/v1.4-enemy-behaviour-trees]]). Pushes `ChangeModelCommand` onto the undo stack.

> **Bare-string texture on the load path.** `entry['texture']` from `level.json` is assigned directly to `Entity.texture` at four sites (`main.py:168`, and `Scripts/level_editor.py` `_restore_editor_level`/`_spawn_gameplay_from_snapshot`/`load_existing_level`) — the same *shape* as the [[brain/Gotchas]] Step-4 model bug. It works today because Ursina's `load_texture` recursively globs the asset folders and resolves a bare filename (verified headless: `floor_stone.png` loads the real 64×64 image; `''` → no texture, no crash). It is **not** routed through `_resolve_texture` the way every model load site is routed through `_resolve_model`. data.description: Pluggable behaviour tree system replacing single-behaviour enemies — composable patrol/attack/flee/idle nodes, named presets, level.json config, no subclassing required for common variants

structure.context: Blocked on: [[work/archive/2026/v1.3-asset-import-pipeline]]. See [[work/archive/version-map]] for sequencing.
Blocked on: [[work/archive/2026/v1.3-asset-import-pipeline]]. See [[work/archive/version-map]] for sequencing.

Replace the single-behaviour enemy (detect → shoot) with a composable behaviour tree system. Each enemy owns a top-down evaluated tree that is ticked once per frame. Built-in leaf nodes cover patrol, chase, attack, flee, and idle. Composition is handled by standard combinators. Trees are configured per-type in `level.json` and `mod.json` — no Python subclassing needed for common enemy variants. This feature was unblocked by the v1.1 fix that wired `CollisionManager.query_layer()` (see [[work/audits/2026-05-20-full-audit]]).
`mod.json` enemy types include a `"behaviour"` field using the same preset-name syntax (see [[work/active/v2.0-release]]).

- **`PatrolNode` movement** uses the same wall-avoidance approach as `player_controller.py:_swept_blocked` — extract to a shared utility or import directly rather than duplicating raycast logic (see [[brain/Patterns]] for the no-duplication rule)
> sources in [[brain/Key Decisions]], [[brain/Patterns]], and [[brain/Gotchas]].

Ownership contract (one tree per enemy, per-instance node state allowed), stateless RUNNING re-evaluation, and `Parallel`'s non-all-succeed aggregate all documented in the module — see [[brain/Key Decisions]].)

4. `PatrolNode` — requires waypoint routing; test with a simple two-point path — **DONE** (2026-06-30, date approximate; `Scripts/behaviour_nodes.py`. `PATROL_WAYPOINT_THRESHOLD` derived named constant; loops to index 0; empty-waypoint raises at construction; Vec3 duck-typed — see [[brain/Patterns]].)

5. `FleeNode` — requires HP threshold check — **DONE** (2026-06-30, date approximate; `Scripts/behaviour_nodes.py`. `flee_range` = target separation distance (not travel budget); reuses inverted `chase_step`; oscillation documented — see [[brain/Key Decisions]].)

6. Decorators: `Invert`, `Repeat`, `Cooldown` — **DONE** (2026-06-30, date approximate; `Scripts/behaviour_tree.py`. Unit-tested (`tests/test_behaviour_tree_decorators.py`, 17 cases) but **not used by any shipping preset** — known gap logged in [[brain/Key Decisions]].)

8. `level.json` `"behaviour"` field support in `load_level()` (main.py) and `load_existing_level()` (level_editor.py) — **DONE** (2026-06-30, date approximate. See [[brain/Gotchas]] and [[brain/Patterns]].)
